title: Intro: Where to start in trading? Part 1
subtitle: Trader role, spot, futures, Binance, P2P
outcome: Understand spot vs futures and how to start safely on exchange.
content: Trading is probability work with defined risk.
takeaway: Traders earn through discipline and repeatable setups.

sections.body:
A trader buys and sells assets to profit from price moves. Horizon: seconds to weeks. Tools: chart, order book, risk per trade.

Pros know entry, stop, target before clicking. Amateurs hope.

Spot = you own the coin. Futures USDT-M = contract with leverage, shorts, liquidation risk.

Terminals (TradingView, exchange UI) speed up execution.

sections.title: Your market (Mexico / LATAM)
sections.body: En México muchos entran por P2P USDT y Mercado Pago. Binance y Bitso son comunes. Regla CNBV: educación sí, asesoría personalizada no sin licencia.

sections.title: Spot vs futures
sections.caption: Spot = ownership. Futures = leveraged contract.

sections.title: TradingView: Binance BTC setup
sections.body: Open BTC chart. Mark yesterday's high/low. Compare spot vs futures price.

sections.title: Hands-on practice
sections.body: 15 minutes observation only:
items: Open Binance or TradingView paper account.
Compare BTC spot vs perpetual futures price.
Draw horizontal lines on yesterday high/low.
Write where your stop would be for a long.

sections.title: Scenario
sections.body: 500 USDT, 5x long ETH: +2% = +10% on margin. No stop = liquidation risk on dump.

sections.title: Remember
items: P2P + KYC for funding in LATAM
Trade Master teaches setups, not signals

sections.title: Trade journal
sections.body: Record after lesson:
items: Deposit size and risk % per trade

sections.title: Pro tip
sections.body: Explore the UI before first trade. Finera teaches savings — we teach chart setups with TradingView.

title: Intro: Where to start? Part 2
subtitle: Intro: Where to start? Part 2

content:
Exchange trading: limit/market orders, maker/taker fees, futures funding.

Tiger.Trade and Vataga gi

sections.body:
Exchange trading: limit/market orders, maker/taker fees, futures funding.

Tiger.Trade and Vataga give order book, hotkeys — essential for scalping.
Scalping (seconds–minutes), swing (hours–days), position (weeks). First deposit = affordable loss. Risk 0.5–1% per trade.

title: Price amplitude
subtitle: Price amplitude
outcome: Break down 'Price amplitude' on chart and TradingView.
content:
Amplitude = move size in %. On BTC 1% is large on 1h; on low-cap alts 10%/hour is common.

Measure w

sections.body:
Amplitude = move size in %. On BTC 1% is large on 1h; on low-cap alts 10%/hour is common.

Measure with ATR or candle range. Size targets to amplitude.
High amplitude → reduce leverage. Low → don't expect huge intraday targets.

sections.title: LATAM market
sections.body: Altcoins en México siguen a BTC con beta alto.

sections.title: Price amplitude
sections.caption: High amplitude → reduce leverage. Low → don't expect huge intraday targets.

sections.title: TradingView
sections.body: On 15m mark range of last 20 candles in %. That's your amplitude baseline for targets.

title: Support by lows
subtitle: Support by lows
outcome: Break down 'Support by lows' on chart and TradingView.
content:
Support by lows: horizontal through swing lows with 2+ bounces.

More touches = stronger level. Fals

sections.body:
Support by lows: horizontal through swing lows with 2+ bounces.

More touches = stronger level. False break below low often signals long.
Build from bodies or wicks — stay consistent. 5m and 1h levels differ.

sections.body: Competitors don't link lesson → TradingView → journal. You get the full loop.
